content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 2 correctness regressions. The new live/audit root selection hides requirements and milestone artifacts after `init` because those stores were moved under `.git/ralph-burning-live` without mirroring, and a targeted CLI test now fails. Separately, fully-qualified planner bead ids are misclassified as implicit, so metadata-only rematerializations can incorrectly clear milestone lineage and progress.

## Amendments

1. src/adapters/fs.rs:408-415 makes `workspace_root_path()` prefer `.git/ralph-burning-live` whenever `init` has created it, and milestone/requirements storage now follows that root (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2354-2358`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:4244-4245`). `initialize_workspace()` always creates the live root (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/mod.rs:35-57`), but there is no seeding or mirroring path for requirements runs or milestone artifacts. That breaks the existing on-disk contract: data written under `.ralph-burning/requirements` or `.ralph-burning/milestones` becomes invisible after init, and new plan artifacts stop landing in the audited tree. I reproduced this with `cargo test --test cli project_create_from_requirements_materializes_milestone_bundle_output`, which now fails with `requirements run not found`. Keep milestone/requirements data in `.ralph-burning`, or add the same live/audit seeding+mirroring logic that projects now use.
   Rationale: This is a compatibility regression in the core milestone handoff path: planner output and materialized plan artifacts are no longer reliably discoverable after workspace initialization.
2. `infer_bead_explicit_id()` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:403-419` treats any fully-qualified bead id that matches the canonical implicit slot as `explicit_id = false`. That is not just a legacy shape: the planner stub itself emits qualified ids like `ms-stub.bead-1` (`src/adapters/stub_backend.rs:737-747`). Once misclassified, `progress_shape_signature()` includes title/workstream metadata for that bead (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:357-389`), and `persist_plan_locked()` will clear lineage/progress on a metadata-only rematerialization (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:622-636`). Qualified explicit ids need to stay explicit; otherwise an innocuous rename of an explicitly identified bead can wipe completion state. Add a regression test that rematerializes a bundle whose planner-supplied id is already `milestone.bead-1` and changes only descriptive fields.
   Rationale: The current inference conflates explicit qualified ids with legacy implicit ids, which can silently destroy milestone progress on normal planner updates.
